pr.title: ftMewtwo SpecialS.c and SpecialLw.c complete - matching
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Decompiled and wired Mewtwo's Side Special Confusion and Down Special Disable into C, removed large corresponding chunks from ftmewtwo.s, and added supporting Mewtwo neutral-special helper code. The PR also brought in a mostly complete Mr. Game & Watch Chef implementation, with the first sausage-spawn function kept as inline asm behind a NON_MATCHING split; the PR body explicitly notes it builds without NON_MATCHING=1. Header and state-var work named previously anonymous Mewtwo and Game & Watch fields, added action-state preservation flags, and updated item/projectile function signatures used by Mewtwo's Disable and Shadow Ball interactions.
what_changed: Added new C source for Mewtwo Confusion in ftMewtwo_SpecialS.c, including grounded/aerial StartAction, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, ground-to-air and air-to-ground transitions, grab/victim handling, reflect setup, ReflectThink, and OnReflect.

Added new C source for Mewtwo Disable in ftMewtwo_SpecialLw.c, including projectile GObj clear/remove helpers, grounded/aerial StartAction, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, ground-to-air and air-to-ground transitions, and Disable projectile creation.
Added ftMewtwo_SpecialN.c helper functions for Shadow Ball charge-state queries and cleanup, including ftMewtwo_SpecialN_GetChargeLevel, func_80146D24, func_80146D74, and func_80146DC8.

Reduced asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s substantially, deleting the old assembly bodies for SpecialS and SpecialLw routines and leaving action-state table references updated to C symbols such as ftMewtwo_SpecialS_Anim, ftMewtwo_SpecialAirS_Coll, ftMewtwo_SpecialLw_Anim, and ftMewtwo_SpecialAirLw_Coll.

Renamed Mewtwo special attribute fields in fighter.h from anonymous x222C/x2238/x223C to x222C_disableGObj, x2238_shadowBallGObj, and x223C_isConfusionBoost, improving documentation of Disable, Shadow Ball, and Confusion state.
Updated ftmewtwo.c death/damage callbacks so ftMewtwo_OnDeath2 and ftMewtwo_OnTakeDamage remove Disable via ftMewtwo_SpecialLw_RemoveDisable before calling existing cleanup functions.

Expanded ftmewtwo.h with Mewtwo SpecialS and SpecialLw prototypes, action-state preservation flag macros, Confusion reflect flag constants, required includes, and corrected Disable attributes x78/x7C to gravity and terminal velocity naming.
Added ftMewtwoSpecialS state vars with bitfield isConfusionReflect in ftMewtwo_StateVars.h.

Added a complete ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c implementation for Chef callbacks, loop transitions, sausage creation logic, and ground/air state changes; the first function ftGameWatch_SpecialN_CreateSausage is explicitly kept as asm outside NON_MATCHING because the C version is non-matching.
Added ftGameWatchSpecialN state vars isChefLoopDisable and maxSausage, plus FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALN_COLL_FLAG and Chef prototypes in Game & Watch headers.
Updated obj_files.mk to compile the new Mewtwo SpecialLw, SpecialS, SpecialN and Game & Watch SpecialN C objects.
Adjusted item-side declarations/signatures and asm branch targets so item code can call Mewtwo helpers such as ftMewtwo_SpecialLw_ClearDisableGObj and ftMewtwo_SpecialN_GetChargeLevel.
smart_moves: Kept the non-matching Game & Watch sausage creation routine isolated with an #ifdef NON_MATCHING C version and an asm fallback, preserving normal builds while documenting the attempted C reconstruction.
Moved callback target names from address-like labels to descriptive fighter-special names in action-state tables, making future table audits easier.
Used small inline helpers for repeated state setup, such as Mewtwo grab setup, Disable callback reinstallation, Confusion reflect restoration, Game & Watch Chef var setup, and Chef loop checks.
Renamed special-attribute and special-state fields at the same time as the matching work, reducing reliance on raw offsets like 0x222C, 0x2238, 0x223C, 0x2340, and 0x2344.
Preserved projectile/object cleanup paths by hooking Disable removal into Mewtwo death and damage callbacks rather than only handling it at animation end.
Annotated many decompiled functions with original addresses and decomp.me scratch links, giving future matchers concrete provenance for each routine.
Added object-file build integration in the same PR as asm deletion, which is necessary for matching decomp slices to replace assembly safely.
decomp_lessons: When replacing fighter special assembly, update the action-state table function pointers and obj_files.mk together; otherwise the C callbacks may exist but not be used or built.
State-var unions are a useful place to convert raw x2340/x2344 usage into named per-move fields, but each special move sharing the union must be checked for overlap.

Projectile-owning specials need cleanup callbacks on death/take-damage as well as animation completion; Mewtwo Disable stores its projectile in x222C_disableGObj and clears/removes it through helper functions.

For difficult functions that do not match under the current compiler setup, a documented NON_MATCHING C attempt plus asm fallback can allow progress without requiring NON_MATCHING=1 for normal builds.

Action-state transition flags should be named per move, such as FTMEWTWO_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG, FTMEWTWO_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG, and FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALN_COLL_FLAG, rather than duplicating long bitwise flag expressions throughout callbacks.

Field naming should encode gameplay meaning when known: x223C_isConfusionBoost, x2238_shadowBallGObj, x222C_disableGObj, isConfusionReflect, isChefLoopDisable, and maxSausage are more reusable than anonymous offsets.

Ground/air special pairs commonly mirror structure: StartAction, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, GroundToAir, AirToGround, and sometimes Loop helpers. Keeping that order in headers and source improves reviewability.
naming_conventions: Mewtwo move files use ftMewtwo_SpecialS.c, ftMewtwo_SpecialLw.c, and ftMewtwo_SpecialN.c, with functions named ftMewtwo_SpecialS_* and ftMewtwo_SpecialAirS_* for grounded/aerial variants.
Game & Watch Chef functions use ftGameWatch_SpecialN_* and ftGameWatch_SpecialAirN_* names, replacing raw labels such as lbl_8014E4F0 with ftGameWatch_SpecialN_CreateSausage.
Action-state preservation flag macros are move-scoped and all-caps, for example FTMEWTWO_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG, FTMEWTWO_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG, and FTGAMEWATCH_SPECIALN_COLL_FLAG.
Confusion reflect ftcmd states are named as CONFUSION_REFLECT_NONE, CONFUSION_REFLECT_ON, and CONFUSION_REFLECT_OFF.
Per-character state vars are nested under union members matching the move, such as mewtwoVars[0].SpecialS.isConfusionReflect and gameWatchVars[0].SpecialN.maxSausage.
SpecialAttrs_Mewtwo fields gained gameplay-oriented suffixes: x222C_disableGObj, x2234_shadowBallCharge, x2238_shadowBallGObj, and x223C_isConfusionBoost.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed large assembly bodies from ftmewtwo.s and ftgamewatch.s once corresponding C implementations and action-state references were in place.
Used inline asm fallback for ftGameWatch_SpecialN_CreateSausage outside NON_MATCHING because the C reconstruction of the first Chef function was still non-matching.
Used #pragma peephole off/on around NON_MATCHING item helper C attempts in item2.c, while retaining asm implementations for normal builds.
Inserted small float-return helper functions in ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c, apparently to influence float constant ordering for matching.
Preserved exact callback shapes with empty IASA and OnReflect functions that simply return, rather than simplifying them away.
Carried over original addresses and decomp.me scratch links in comments to tie each C function to the replaced assembly routine.

Kept some suspicious-looking duplicate assignments such as 'Fighter* fp = fp = getFighter(...)' in several functions, likely as a matching/codegen tactic; evidence indicates this was present in the submitted diff, but no reviewer explanation is available.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the supplied PR slice.
The PR body notes an important limitation: ftGameWatch_SpecialN.c is included completely, but its first function is non-matching.
The PR body states the branch builds without the NON_MATCHING=1 flag, which is consistent with keeping the problematic Chef function behind an asm fallback.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that make ftGameWatch_SpecialN_CreateSausage matching and remove its asm fallback.
Search later history for cleanup of duplicate assignment matching idioms like 'fp = fp = getFighter'.
Check follow-up Mewtwo PRs for renames of func_80146D24, func_80146D74, func_80146DC8, and item projectile helpers func_802C49E0/func_802C4A40.
Audit whether FTMEWTWO_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG and FTMEWTWO_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG were reused or refined in later Mewtwo matching work.
Search for follow-up review or commits around item2.c NON_MATCHING peephole work for func_8026B924 and func_8026B960.
